name: Path of the Water
name_original: Ruta del agua del río Inglares— circular trailrunning desde Berganzo
description: Run the moderate 5.2-mile Ruta del agua del río Inglares circular trail from Berganzo, featuring waterfalls and river views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Zambrana traverse the historical territory of Álava in Spain's Basque Country, offering diverse natural landscapes. The region features lush green scenery, limestone mountain ranges, and rolling hills, providing varied terrain for runners. Existing hiking trails are well-suited for jogging, often passing through beech and oak forests and offering views of the surrounding mountainous areas. This environment provides ample opportunities for outdoor exercise amidst natural beauty. Best jogging routes around Zambrana The most popular jogging route is Path of the Water, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This route offers scenic river views and refreshing waterfalls. Another top favourite among local runners is Cruz de Motrico and GR 99 Loop, a difficult 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This route explores limestone mountain ranges and offers expansive natural vistas. Local runners also love the Running loop from Ruinas Romanas de Deóbriga, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through lush green scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. Jogging around Zambrana is defined by lush green scenery, limestone mountain ranges, and rolling hills. The network of running routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Zambrana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 32 reviews. More than 1300 runners have used komoot to explore Zambrana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Zambrana?
A: Zambrana offers a wide variety of running routes, with nearly 170 different trails to explore. These range from easy paths to more challenging mountain runs, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Zambrana?
A: The jogging trails in Zambrana traverse diverse landscapes, characterized by lush green scenery, limestone mountain ranges, and rolling hills. You'll find yourself running through deep beech and oak forests, along rivers, and across open areas with impressive natural vistas, especially in the Southern Sierras of Álava.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Zambrana?
A: Yes, Zambrana has several easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. There are 7 easy routes available, offering gentle terrain and pleasant scenery.

Q: Can I find routes with waterfalls or scenic river views?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its natural beauty. For refreshing waterfalls and scenic river views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058768882/path-of-the-water" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of the Water&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route follows the Inglares River and is a local favorite.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Zambrana offers 70 difficult routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050726963/cruz-de-motrico-and-gr-99-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Motrico and GR 99 Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores limestone mountain ranges and provides expansive natural vistas over 8.8 miles.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest I might see while running?
A: Many routes pass by significant natural and historical points. You could encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4199700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Motrico&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2905300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Route&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2985857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also lead near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3223322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toloño&lt;/a&gt; peak or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruinas Romanas de Deóbriga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Zambrana are designed as loops, offering convenient circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Ruinas Romanas de Deóbriga&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that takes you through lush green scenery.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Zambrana?
A: The running routes in Zambrana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 32 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the well-maintained hiking trails suitable for jogging, and the stunning backdrops of the Basque Country's green scenery and limestone mountains.

Q: Are there any longer routes for endurance training?
A: For longer runs, you can find several routes over 14 km. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20506181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berganzo Village – Txulato loop from Berganzo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.7 km route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31511563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txulato – Peña Infinito loop from Lanosko gaztelua&lt;/a&gt; extends to 15.7 km, both offering significant elevation changes.

Q: Is Zambrana a good place for running year-round?
A: Zambrana, located in the Basque Country, generally offers a suitable environment for outdoor activities throughout the year due to its varied terrain and commitment to preserving natural heritage. While specific seasonal conditions vary, the lush green landscapes and established trail networks provide opportunities for running in most seasons.
tips: Simple and spectacular walk following the course of the river. It is worth going to go to the shore on the trails that start from the general path. Especially recommended in autumn and with enough water in the riverbed.
Nice waterfall among thick vegetation, located at the end of the path known as the "Ruta del Agua"

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trailrunning route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot data suggests an average duration of approximately 58 minutes for trailrunning this route. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta del agua del río Inglares suitable for beginners in trailrunning?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally considered to be of easy to moderate difficulty, making it suitable for beginners in trailrunning. While most of the trail is relatively flat, there is at least one short but steep ascent near the Cascada de las Herrerías. The trail is well-marked, which helps with navigation.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring natural paths, some unpaved sections, and parts that coincide with the GR-1 long-distance trail. You'll also run alongside an old hydroelectric canal. Expect a mix of surfaces, with most of the trail being comfortable for running, though there's a notable uphill section.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in natural beauty and historical elements. Key highlights include the Water Route itself, with its numerous waterfalls, rapids, and turquoise pools. A major draw is the impressive Ollerías Waterfall (also known as Cascada de las Herrerías), considered one of the most stunning in the Basque Country. The old hydroelectric canal also offers magnificent panoramic views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run the Ruta del agua del río Inglares?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly splendid due to the dense riverside forests of beech, oak, and holm oak displaying vibrant colors. Spring and early summer also offer lush greenery and strong water flow in the river and waterfalls. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta del agua del río Inglares dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas where dogs are generally welcome, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trail itself. However, it's important to note that parking at the trailhead in Berganzo requires a prior reservation due to limited capacity and to manage visitor numbers for the sustainability of the route. Plan ahead for parking.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route fully encompasses the 'Uraren bidea' trail. It also passes through sections of the 'Ibilbide historikoa: Araba', 'Vuelta a los Payos', and 'Ruta de los Castillos' trails, offering connections to broader networks.

faqs.question: What amenities are available in Berganzo village near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Berganzo is a charming historic village. While specific amenities like cafes or pubs are not detailed, it's a good idea to check local listings for services available before or after your run. The village itself adds to the scenic appeal of the area.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for trailrunning this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is well-marked, but some sections can be uneven or slippery, especially near water features or after rain. Good trailrunning shoes with adequate grip are recommended. Always carry enough water, especially on warmer days, and be mindful of the short but steep ascent near the Cascada de las Herrerías.

faqs.question: Is there an 'insider tip' for experiencing this trail?
faqs.answer: To fully appreciate the natural beauty and historical elements, consider taking a moment at the old hydroelectric canal section. It offers magnificent panoramic views of the surrounding mountains, Berganzo village, Ocio, and the Lanos Castle, providing a different perspective than the riverside path.
